title: Stellar Wave 2 — XDR decoder to semantic facts
goal: Implement ChainAdapter.decode for Stellar: parse a base64 TransactionEnvelope XDR via @stellar/stellar-sdk into the Wave 0 chain-neutral SemanticFacts so the unchanged brain can evaluate Stellar transactions without knowing anything about XDR, stroops, or assets. Add stroop/decimal conversion, a configurable price provider with a stub fallback, and wire it into a StellarChainAdapter.decode sibling of SolanaChainAdapter.

context.precedent: back/services/solana/transactions/ (Solana tx decoding/payload modules) and docs/wave-6-signer-adapter/ (spec conventions)

openQuestions.question: How should issued assets be valued in USD on testnet, where no reliable live price feed exists?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Start with a stub/allowlist price provider behind config, analogous to FALLBACK_SOL_USD_PRICE. Native XLM uses FALLBACK_XLM_USD_PRICE when live pricing is unavailable. Issued assets without a known price return null/stub from stellarPriceProvider; the policy engine treats an unknown amountUsd conservatively. Live issued-asset pricing is deferred to a later wave.

openQuestions.question: How should a multi-operation TransactionEnvelope be represented to the policy engine — one candidate per envelope with worst-case risk, or one candidate per operation?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: One per-envelope SemanticFacts candidate. The decoder surfaces all operations in order via operations[], and the candidate escalates to the worst-case risk if ANY operation is critical. This keeps the brain consuming a single neutral candidate per submitted transaction (matching the Solana model) while preserving per-operation detail for audit. Policy-engine consumption of the per-operation detail and the escalation rule is documented for and implemented in Wave 3.

acceptanceCriteria: stellarTransactionDecoder parses a base64 TransactionEnvelope XDR into SemanticFacts consumable by the UNCHANGED policy engine.
Stroop/decimal conversion is correct at 7 decimals (10,000,000 stroops = 1 XLM), distinct from Solana lamports (9 decimals).
Native XLM and issued assets (code + issuer) both map cleanly to the neutral SemanticFacts.asset field.
Multi-operation and path-payment envelopes are decoded in full — ALL operations are surfaced in order, not just the first.
Malformed or non-parseable XDR is rejected with a clear decode error and produces no partial or ambiguous SemanticFacts.
A multi-operation envelope is represented as one per-envelope candidate that escalates to worst-case risk if any operation is critical (documented for Wave 3).
stellarPriceProvider maps amount to amountUsd behind a configurable provider with a stub fallback (FALLBACK_XLM_USD_PRICE) analogous to Solana.
StellarChainAdapter.decode returns the Wave 0 SemanticFacts type and is resolvable through chainRegistry for ChainId 'stellar'.
The brain is untouched: policy engine, LLM judge, decision sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS semantics, and the MCP proxy.
Solana keeps working unchanged and all existing Solana tests stay green.
nonGoals: No change to Solana decoding or Solana behavior
No change to the brain (policy engine, LLM judge, decision sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS semantics, MCP proxy)
No ChainAdapter.cosign, submit, or inspectAccount implementation — decode only
No production-grade USD valuation for issued assets (testnet stub/allowlist only)
No new MCP tools and no signer behavior change
verification: Manual: decode a base64 XDR of a testnet native-XLM payment and confirm SemanticFacts { actionKind: 'transfer', recipientAddress, asset: 'XLM', amount, amountUsd }
Manual: decode an issued-asset payment and confirm asset carries code + issuer
Manual: decode a multi-operation envelope and confirm all operations are surfaced in order as one per-envelope candidate
Manual: pass malformed/non-base64 XDR and confirm a clear decode error with no partial fact

tasks.title: Add RED stroop/decimal conversion tests
tasks.description: Write failing tests for stellarAmount stroop <-> display conversion before implementation.
testsSpec: Sub-unit stroop amounts convert to the correct 7-decimal display string
Round-trip stroops -> display -> stroops is lossless with no float drift
The divisor is 10^7 and differs from the Solana lamport divisor (10^9)
No legacy/ import appears in the new stellarAmount file

tasks.title: Implement stellarAmount conversion module
tasks.description: Create stellarAmount.ts converting stroops <-> display units at 7 decimals using string/bigint math (no floating point).

tasks.title: Add RED price provider tests
tasks.description: Write failing tests for stellarPriceProvider amount -> amountUsd mapping and stub fallback before implementation.
testsSpec: amountToUsd returns the configured price for native XLM
amountToUsd falls back to FALLBACK_XLM_USD_PRICE when no live price is available
amountToUsd returns null/stub for an unknown issued asset
No legacy/ import appears in the new stellarPriceProvider file

tasks.title: Implement stellarPriceProvider with stub fallback
tasks.description: Create stellarPriceProvider.ts behind config with a stub/allowlist provider and FALLBACK_XLM_USD_PRICE fallback. Add FALLBACK_XLM_USD_PRICE to .env.example.

tasks.title: Add RED XDR decoder tests
tasks.description: Write failing tests for stellarTransactionDecoder across native, issued, multi-op, and malformed inputs before implementation.
testsSpec: Native-XLM payment XDR decodes to SemanticFacts { actionKind: 'transfer', recipientAddress, asset: 'XLM', amount, amountUsd }
Issued-asset payment XDR decodes with asset carrying code + issuer
Multi-operation / path-payment envelope surfaces ALL operations in order as one per-envelope candidate
Malformed / non-base64 / non-envelope input returns { ok: false, reason: 'MALFORMED_XDR' } with no partial fact
Source account and memo are carried into SemanticFacts
No legacy/ import appears in the new decoder file

tasks.title: Implement stellarTransactionDecoder
tasks.description: Create stellarTransactionDecoder.ts parsing a base64 TransactionEnvelope via @stellar/stellar-sdk into SemanticFacts: source account, ordered operations (all of them), per-payment destination/asset/amount, memo, and amountUsd via stellarPriceProvider. Fail safe on malformed XDR.

tasks.title: Wire decoder into StellarChainAdapter.decode
tasks.description: Implement StellarChainAdapter.decode (sibling to SolanaChainAdapter) delegating to stellarTransactionDecoder, returning the Wave 0 SemanticFacts type, and resolvable through chainRegistry for ChainId 'stellar'.

tasks.title: Validate and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite and stop for parent review. Confirm the brain is untouched and all Solana tests stay green before proceeding to Wave 3.

implementationEvidence.mode: XDR decode -> Wave 0 SemanticFacts; additive; brain & Solana untouched
filesEdited: back/services/chain/__tests__/chainRegistry.test.ts (stellar now resolves; not-registered tested via cast)
commands: npm run test:back -> 354 passed (37 files), incl. 18 new Stellar/chain tests
npm run test:back -- back/services/stellar back/services/chain -> 42 passed
tsc --noEmit -> only the 1 PRE-EXISTING mcpProxyDispatcher error remains; zero new errors
deviations: Decoder uses TransactionBuilder.fromXDR(base64, networkPassphrase) (high-level) rather than raw xdr.TransactionEnvelope.fromXDR; op.amount already arrives as a 7-decimal display string. stellarAmount provides canonical stroop<->display conversion (10^7) and the SemanticFacts.amount number at the edge.

Repo targets ES2017 + strict:false: avoided BigInt literals (used BigInt(...)) and cannot narrow the ok:false union variant (cast the failure variant in adapter; toMatchObject in tests). No tsconfig change (out of scope).

Fee-bump envelopes are unwrapped to innerTransaction. Multi-op envelopes produce one per-envelope candidate (riskFlags: multi_operation + op:<type>); worst-case escalation / critical-op mapping is Wave 3.
Issued assets have no testnet USD price (stub) -> amountUsd 0 with riskFlag amount_usd_unknown (never silent). Native XLM uses FALLBACK_XLM_USD_PRICE.
Real-network XDR/funding integration deferred to Wave 6; decoder tests build envelopes in-process with the SDK (offline/deterministic).
